title: Ramoji Film City
tagline: Ramoji Film City — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Ramoji Film City is an integrated film studio facility located right outside of Hyderabad, India.
overview.description: Ramoji Film City is an integrated film studio facility located right outside of Hyderabad, India. Spread over 2,000 acres (810 ha), it was recognized as the world's largest film studio complex by the Guinness World Records in 2005. Established in 1996 by Telugu media proprietor Ramoji Rao, it has been described as a "city within a city" by The Guardian. In addition to serving as a major hub for film production, Ramoji Film City is a popular thematic holiday destination, featuring a mix of natural landscapes, artificial sets, and an amusement park.

topPlaces.name: Sanghi Nagar
topPlaces.description: Sanghi Nagar is suburb of Hyderabad, India. Sanghi Nagar is an industrial complex set up by the Sanghi Group of Industries.

topPlaces.name: Sanghi Temple
topPlaces.description: Sanghi Temple, which is said to be the replica of Tirumala temple is located at Sanghi Nagar in Telangana in India, is about 35 km from Hyderabad city. The sacred Raja Gopuram, which is very tall, can be seen from several kilometers away.

topPlaces.name: Anajpur
topPlaces.description: Anajpur is a village in Rangareddy district in Telangana, India. It falls under Hayathnagar mandal and is close to the Outer Ring Road, Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Abdullapurmet
topPlaces.description: Abdullapur and Abdullapurmet together constitute a village and mandal in Ranga Reddy district in Telangana, India. Abdullapur is a village whereas Abdullapurmet is Mandal.

topPlaces.name: Batasingaram
topPlaces.description: Batasingaram is a village in Ranga Reddy district in Telangana, India. It falls under Abdullapur mandal.

topPlaces.name: Sheriguda
topPlaces.description: Sheriguda is a village in the Indian state of Telangana. It is in the Rangareddy district.

topPlaces.name: Moula Ali hill
topPlaces.description: Moula Ali hill also known as Maula Ali, had the ancient name dome-shaped hill located in Moula Ali, Hyderabad, India. It is well known for the Moula Ali dargah and shia masjid, which are both on top of the hill.

topPlaces.name: Kawadipally
topPlaces.description: Kawadipally/కవాడిపల్లి is a village in Ranga Reddy district in Telangana, India. It falls under Abdullapurmet mandal where Telugu is the state language.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ramoji Film City?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ramoji Film City is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ramoji Film City?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ramoji Film City?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ramoji Film City at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ramoji Film City?
faq.a: Stays in Ramoji Film City start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ramoji Film City?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sanghi Nagar, Sanghi Temple, Anajpur, Abdullapurmet, Batasingaram.
